title: Proactive monitoring – Natural health products: Health product advertising incidents - Canada.ca
content:
## Health product advertising incidents: Proactive monitoring – Natural health products
- How to report a complaint
- Proactive monitoring – COVID-19
- Proactive monitoring – Natural health products
Natural health products are substances or combinations of substances used to restore or maintain good health.
A Government of Canada audit in 2021 found that Health Canada monitored advertisements in response to complaints instead of monitoring the market using a risk-based approach.
- Report on natural health products by the Commissioner of the Environment and Sustainable Development
In response to this audit, Health Canada undertook an assessment to determine the feasibility of implementing a proactive monitoring surveillance of natural health product advertisements. To assess feasibility, Health Canada launched two pilots.
A web scraping tool was used to search online content, including websites, e-commerce platforms, and social media, for natural health product advertisements making claims related to serious diseases (e.g. diseases listed in Schedule A.1 of the Food and Drugs Act). From June 2021 to December 2021, the focus was on advertising incidents related to unauthorized cancer-related claims. The focus shifted to unauthorized claims related to obesity, acute anxiety, and severe depression from February 2023 to October 2023.
Health Canada has addressed all incidents through the appropriate compliance and enforcement actions, and they are now considered resolved.
The following table summarizes these non-compliant natural health product advertising incidents.
Including a product in this table does not mean the manufacturer was involved in the non-compliant advertising or that the advertising materials originated from the third-party seller.
If you have questions about the incidents in the summary table, email us at drug-device-marketing@hc-sc.gc.ca .
